message:
fix(core,obs,vdbe): reject writes on readonly connections, propagate reload errors, rustfmt cleanups

core: attach + autocommit rollback correctness
-----------------------------------------------
* `Connection` — the schema-mutation helper that previously trusted
  its caller now explicitly rejects writes against a readonly pager
  with `FrankenError::ReadOnly` before touching any transaction
  state. This closes a window where a readonly connection could
  attempt a same-connection DDL and get a later, more confusing
  error from the transaction layer.
* Attach path — drops the `self.pager.is_readonly() && path != ":memory:"`
  branch that used to call `Self::open_schema_only_with_env`. In
  practice, `open_with_env` already picks the right open mode based
  on the attach environment, and the schema-only branch could yield
  a half-open attached connection with missing metadata. Unifying on
  `Self::open_with_env` is simpler and matches the non-readonly path.
* Retained-autocommit flush failure path — the previous commit
  (`fix(core): roll retained-autocommit flush back and reload memdb
  mirror on commit failure`) silently swallowed
  `reload_memdb_from_pager` errors: if the commit failed, we called
  `reload_memdb_from_pager`, ignored its result, and still returned
  the original `commit_error`. Now the match explicitly propagates
  the reload error when it fails, so a broken memdb rehydration
  surfaces as the final error instead of being masked by a stale
  commit error.

obs/connection_pool: rustfmt sweep
----------------------------------
Pure formatting pass over `connection_pool.rs` — rustfmt reflowed
several multi-arg calls in `ConnectionPoolValidator::validate`
(`detect_over_pooling`, `detect_stale_snapshots`,
`recommendation_for`) onto the block form, and adjusted the
`total_queries` sum expression to canonical indentation. No logic
changes.

vdbe: annotate test-only cold-state helper
------------------------------------------
`StatementColdState::contains` is only used by the unit-test module
after the cold-state migration. Added `#[cfg(test)]` + `#[allow(dead_code)]`
so the `-D warnings` build no longer flags it as unused, without
deleting the helper (the test still needs it).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
